title: Anti-Cheat Security Measures
description: Comprehensive anti-cheat security framework covering defense in depth (4 layers: client integrity, server authority, behavioral analysis, enforcement), client build integrity verification, memory protection, network protection, server-side input validation, rate limiting, state consistency, anomaly detection (statistical and ML), and graduated enforcement with appeals.
anti_patterns: trusting client-side game state as authoritative
running critical game logic on the client
no server-side validation of player inputs
single-layer defense relying only on client integrity checks
automated bans based solely on ML model output without corroboration
no rate limiting allowing script-driven input floods
storing decrypted game state in a single contiguous memory region
skipping integrity re-verification during runtime allowing DLL injection
implementation_steps: Establish the four-layer defense in depth model: Layer 1 - Client Integrity (verify unmodified client), Layer 2 - Server Authority (all authoritative state and calculations server-side, client is rendering and input only), Layer 3 - Behavioral Analysis (detect statistically anomalous behavior), Layer 4 - Enforcement (graduated consequences from warnings to permanent bans)

Implement client build integrity: sign each game build with a private key, verify signature at launch before server connection; hash-verify critical game assets (config files, scripts, shaders) at startup with full asset re-download on mismatch; run continuous background integrity checks re-verifying loaded modules every 60 seconds to detect runtime patching (DLL injection, memory modification)

Implement memory protection: anti-debugging techniques (hardware breakpoints, software breakpoints, debugger attachment detection triggering immediate disconnect); protect critical memory regions (game state, network buffers, encryption keys) using virtual memory protection APIs and periodic integrity checks; fragment game state across memory with obfuscated pointers instead of storing in a single contiguous region

Implement network protection: TLS 1.3 for all client-server communication plus application-level encryption (AES-256-GCM with per-session keys) to prevent packet sniffing and replay; include packet sequence numbers and timestamps in encrypted payload to detect injection, reordering, and replay; server validates command rate and order, rejecting flooding and out-of-order sequences

Implement server-side input validation: validate all client inputs against current game state; use server-side physics simulation with tolerance window for movement (correct deviations, flag persistent cheats); validate action inputs against cooldown timers, resource requirements, and line-of-sight maintained server-side

Implement rate limiting and throttling: rate-limit per command type calibrated slightly above theoretical maximum for skilled human players; burst allowances for short intense periods with slow replenishment; global command budget limiting total commands per minute per session to prevent script-driven floods

Implement server-side state consistency: server is single source of truth, client updates are suggestions validated before applying; log critical state transitions (inventory changes, currency transactions, level progression) with before/after snapshots; run periodic consistency checks reconciling inventory counts with acquisition/consumption logs and currency balances with transaction histories, flagging discrepancies for investigation

Implement statistical anomaly detection: calculate coefficient of variation of action intervals flagging players below 1st percentile (automated tools have unusually low timing variance); track performance metrics (accuracy, reaction time, resource acquisition rate) per session flagging performance exceeding 5 standard deviations above mean for sustained periods; detect unnatural play patterns (24-hour continuous play, perfectly repetitive sequences, inhuman response times)

Implement ML-based detection: unsupervised Isolation Forest model trained on behavioral feature vectors to identify anomalous overall profiles; supervised model trained on labeled cheat/non-cheat data classifying flagged players; retrain monthly with human-reviewed cases; ML predictions are one input to enforcement decision only, never sole basis for automated action without corroborating evidence from other layers

Implement player reporting system: players report suspected cheaters; reports weighted by reporter credibility (historical accuracy); aggregate reports to identify investigation targets; high-volume reports against single player trigger expedited moderation review

Implement graduated enforcement framework: Level 1 Warning (first minor anomaly, low confidence, permanent record), Level 2 Shadow Ban (repeated minor anomalies or moderate confidence, 7 days, matched only with other shadow-banned players, not notified), Level 3 Competitive Suspension (confirmed cheat in competitive mode, 30 days), Level 4 Account Suspension (confirmed cheat with economic impact, 90 days), Level 5 Permanent Ban (confirmed cheat with severe impact or repeated Level 4, permanent with hardware ID blacklist requiring VP approval)
Implement appeals process: players can appeal Level 3+ actions through support ticket; senior moderator reviews within 72 hours; successful appeals restore account and remove strike from record

Implement cheat incident response: document exploit and assess impact (affected features, user count, economic damage); deploy server-side mitigation within 4 hours for critical exploits; update client-side detection in next patch; identify affected players through forensic analysis and apply enforcement; conduct post-incident review for prevention and architectural improvements
rules: Never trust the client: all authoritative game state and calculations must be performed server-side; the client is a rendering and input layer only
All game logic affecting competitive fairness or economic integrity must be protected by these anti-cheat measures
Client builds must be signed with a private key; modified binaries must fail signature verification and cannot connect to the server
Continuous integrity re-verification every 60 seconds to detect runtime patching (DLL injection, memory modification)
Anti-debugging techniques must be employed: detect hardware breakpoints, software breakpoints, and debugger attachment with immediate disconnect
Game state must be fragmented across memory with obfuscated pointers; never store decrypted state in a single contiguous memory region
All client-server communication must use TLS 1.3 plus application-level AES-256-GCM encryption with per-session keys
Packet sequence numbers and timestamps must be included in encrypted payload to detect injection, reordering, and replay
All client inputs must be validated server-side against current game state including movement physics, cooldown timers, resource requirements, and line-of-sight
Rate limiting must be calibrated slightly above theoretical maximum for skilled human players with global command budget per session
Server is the single source of truth for all game state; client state updates are suggestions validated before applying
Critical state transitions must be logged with before/after snapshots and validated against business rules
Periodic consistency checks must reconcile inventory and currency balances against transaction logs
ML predictions must never be the sole basis for automated enforcement action; corroboration from other layers is required
Shadow-banned players must be matched only with other shadow-banned players and their actions must not affect the legitimate economy
Hardware ID bans require VP-level approval before implementation
Appeals for Level 3+ actions must be reviewed by a senior moderator within 72 hours
Server-side mitigations for critical exploits must be deployed within 4 hours

examples.name: Movement Validation
examples.description: Server maintains physics simulation; client reports position; server checks deviation from calculated position within tolerance window; persistent deviations beyond threshold trigger cheat flag and server-side correction

examples.name: Automated Tool Detection via Timing
examples.description: Human players have natural variance in input timing (coefficient of variation typically 0.3-0.5); automated tools produce patterns with CV below 0.05; system flags players below 1st percentile of CV distribution

examples.name: Shadow Ban Implementation
examples.description: Player flagged for repeated anomalies is shadow-banned: they continue playing normally but are matched exclusively with other shadow-banned players; their marketplace trades and economic actions do not affect the legitimate economy; they are not notified of the restriction

examples.name: Value Disparity Fraud Detection
examples.description: Statistical analysis tracks resource acquisition rates; players exceeding 5 standard deviations above the mean for sustained periods are flagged; combined with supervised ML model output and server-side state consistency checks, the system builds a multi-layered case before enforcement

examples.name: Cheat Incident Response
examples.description: New exploit discovered → document and assess impact → deploy server-side mitigation within 4 hours → update client detection in next patch → identify affected players via forensic analysis → apply graduated enforcement → post-incident review for architectural improvements
